summary: Phase 4 backend testing complete. Added 23 new tests covering: expanded catalog (18 products w/ brand+supplier), public order tracking (5 cases incl. missing/empty/wrong email + unknown id), marketing channels (admin/non-admin/anon), campaign CRUD + send (graceful no-op for telegram/facebook/instagram, 404, channel filtering, empty channels 400), and notification settings Meta fields (fb_page_id unmasked, fb/ig tokens masked, telegram_channel_id persisted). All 23 Phase 4 tests PASS. Combined regression run: 84/85 tests pass. The single failure (TestNotifications.test_status_shape in backend_test.py) is a PRE-EXISTING test isolation issue — it asserts telegram chat_id_set=False but the notification_settings DB doc persists across test iterations from Phase 3 runs. API logic is correct; only test cleanup is missing.

minor.issue: Empty string values are filtered out by `if v is not None and v != ''` in notifications_set — cannot clear/reset a previously-set credential via API. Not blocking but worth noting.
Empty email param ('') correctly returns 400, but consider also rejecting whitespace-only. Currently FastAPI passes '' through `if not email` which catches it — OK.

action_items: (Pre-existing, not Phase 4) Either reset notification_settings doc in a session-scoped fixture in backend_test.py, or relax test_status_shape to not assert chat_id_set=False — current assertion fails on second run due to DB state leftover from prior Phase 3 tests setting telegram_chat_id.
Consider adding ability to clear individual notification setting fields (e.g. allow explicit null to unset).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 1285 lines — recommend splitting into routers (auth.py, products.py, orders.py, marketing.py, payments.py) as the file is growing past the 700-line guideline.
Order tracking endpoint correctly excludes user_id and session_id from public response (verified via test_track_correct_id_and_email).
marketing.post_instagram correctly enforces image_url requirement (returns sent:false with reason rather than 500).
Cart endpoints (PUT /cart/update, DELETE /cart/{pid}) have no null-cart guard — `cart.get('items')` on None will throw. Verified via code read; not Phase 4 scope but worth noting.
Phase 4 endpoints all have proper admin guards via Depends(require_admin), input validation via Pydantic, and graceful failure (sent:false + reason) instead of 500 errors.

success_rate.backend: 100% (23/23 Phase 4) | 84/85 combined; the 1 failure is pre-existing DB-state pollution in backend_test.py, not a Phase 4 regression

seed_data_creation: Created a TEST_ customer + pending order via /api/payments/checkout (real Stripe session via emergentintegrations) for the order-tracking test fixture. Test campaigns auto-cleaned via DELETE in test_delete_campaign_admin and test_create_invalid_channels_filtered. Persistent test data: notification_settings.fb_page_id='TEST_fb_page_123', ig_user_id='TEST_ig_999', telegram_channel_id='@TEST_channel' (cannot be cleared via API — see action items).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 4 endpoints verified: expanded catalog (18 products), GET /api/orders/track/{id}?email=, /api/marketing/channels, /api/marketing/campaigns (CRUD + /send), notification settings Meta fields. All graceful no-ops work — telegram/fb/ig all return sent:false with reason when creds missing. The notification_settings doc persists between test runs causing TestNotifications.test_status_shape to fail on re-runs (Phase 3 test in backend_test.py). To fix: add a teardown fixture that resets the notification_settings doc or directly drops it via mongo. NOTE: Phase 4 tests in test_phase4.py set fb/ig/telegram_channel_id in DB — this is intentional for the masking test and also causes marketing channels facebook/instagram to report configured:true subsequently.

rca_of_issue_test_status_shape_failure.reproduction: Run pytest twice — first run passes (or fresh DB), second run fails because earlier TestNotifications tests in same suite set telegram_chat_id which persists in notification_settings doc.
rca_of_issue_test_status_shape_failure.root_cause: No teardown/cleanup of notification_settings collection between test sessions. The doc is upserted on PUT and never reset.
rca_of_issue_test_status_shape_failure.mitigation: Add session-scoped fixture in backend_test.py conftest: db_clear_notification_settings() that drops or resets {id:'global'} before TestNotifications class runs. Alternatively relax assertion to: 'assert isinstance(d["telegram"]["chat_id_set"], bool)'.
